headline: Nintendo confirms it will sell a new Switch 2 with replaceable battery in the EU
summary: Nintendo plans a Switch 2 for the EU that will feature an easily replaceable battery. This design change aims to comply with a new EU regulation. The regulation is set to take effect on February 18th, 2027.

headline: Apple is bringing age verification to Texas this week
summary: Apple will introduce App Store age verification for Texas users starting Thursday, June 4th. This move follows a federal appeals court allowing Texas's App Store Accountability Act to proceed. The new requirement affects users creating accounts in the state.

headline: WiiM expands its whole-home ecosystem with a new soundbar
summary: Audio company WiiM is expanding its whole-home ecosystem with the new WiiM Bar, releasing in July. The soundbar supports various streaming options and offers expandability. It aims to deliver audiophile-level performance at an affordable price.

headline: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer
summary: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips, confirming at least two more RTX Spark generations. CEO Jensen Huang revealed this at Computex 2026. The company's ultimate goal is to achieve a 'Star Trek computer' level of intelligence.

headline: This $1,800 Power Lift Recliner and Massage Chair Is Just $385 Shipped at Wayfair After an 80% Off Discount
summary: An $1,800 power lift recliner and massage chair is currently on sale. It's available for just $385, including shipping, at Wayfair. This represents an 80% discount on the original price.

headline: IO Interactive Won't Self-Publish Future James Bond Games; Amazon Will Have More Control
summary: IO Interactive will no longer self-publish future James Bond game sequels. Amazon and MGM will take over publishing responsibilities for the series. This change grants Amazon greater control over the 007 First Light franchise.

headline: You Can Now Fight Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick in WWE 2K26
summary: Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick is now a playable character in WWE 2K26. Players can step into the ring and fight the gaming executive. This adds a unique and unexpected celebrity fighter to the game's roster.

headline: The FlashForge AD5X Is a CoreXY 3D Printer With Multi-Color, Mixed Material Capability for Under $230
summary: The FlashForge AD5X is a CoreXY 3D printer now available for under $230. It features multi-color and mixed-material printing capabilities. This makes it an excellent option for both beginners and advanced enthusiasts.

headline: Police chief apologises to Henry Nowak's family over handcuffing and arrest
summary: A police chief apologized to Henry Nowak's family for the handcuffing and arrest of the murder victim. Chief Constable Alexis Boon told the BBC the footage of the incident was distressing. The apology addresses concerns over how Nowak was treated by officers.

headline: PM accuses Farage of exploiting Nowak case and denies 'two-tier policing' claim
summary: The Prime Minister accused Nigel Farage of exploiting the Henry Nowak case. He denied Farage's claim of 'two-tier policing' in the UK. Farage had previously urged the public to respond with 'pure, cold rage' to police actions.

headline: US House votes to rein in Trump's war powers in rebuke over Iran
summary: The US House voted to rein in former President Trump's war powers. The measure passed 215-208, with four Republicans joining Democrats. This vote served as a rebuke over Trump's military actions concerning Iran.

headline: What a hair loss breakthrough could mean for women like me
summary: Scientists are nearing significant breakthroughs in hair loss treatments. Victoria Derbyshire explores the potential impact these developments could have on women. These new treatments offer hope for millions affected by hair loss.
